headline.purpose: Section headings across landing and portals.

body.purpose: Running text everywhere; 1.1rem on landing, 1.05rem in portals.

label.purpose: Uppercase section kickers — the only uppercase, only heavy weight in the system.

shadows.name: border
shadows.purpose: Resting card elevation — hairline ring, never a visible border.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Large ambient shadow for hero and top-tier feature panels.

motion.name: ease-ui
motion.purpose: Standard UI transitions — buttons, links, header morph, ~160-220ms.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Reveal/spring-out motion — image warm-ups, card expansion, scroll-in panels, ~380-720ms.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA. Fully round, terracotta fill, darkens and lifts on hover, scales in on press.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 999px; background: #b85d35; color: #fff; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 520; white-space: nowrap; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1), translate 180ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1), scale 180ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #964829; translate: 0 -1px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { translate: 0 0; scale: 0.96; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b85d35; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action. Transparent fill, hairline inset ring instead of a border.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 22px; border-radius: 999px; background: transparent; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(15,23,42,0.13); color: #111827; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 520; white-space: nowrap; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(15,23,42,0.04); }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Accent-tinted pill for tags, counts, and status labels.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; min-height: 28px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(184,93,53,0.08); color: #964829; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.01em; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Neutral Chip
components.description: Informational chip variant without accent color.
components.css: .ds-chip-neutral { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; min-height: 28px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(15,23,42,0.05); color: #64748b; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.01em; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default surface card: white, 24px radius, hairline resting shadow.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 24px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.06), 0 1px 2px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.06), 0 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); padding: 24px; transition: box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 1px 2px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 8px 24px rgba(15,23,42,0.1); }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: The one uppercase element in the system — section kicker.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display: block; margin-bottom: 28px; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.22em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #64748b; }

components.name: Heading with Emphasis
components.description: The italic-accent emphasis rule applied inside a heading.
components.css: h2 { margin: 0; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.06; letter-spacing: -0.02em; font-size: clamp(2rem, 4.2vw, 3.8rem); text-wrap: balance; } h2 em { font-style: italic; color: #b85d35; }

narrative.overview:
Verae Health reads like a premium health magazine, not a SaaS dashboard: a pure-white canvas, generous whitespace, and exactly one warm accent color, with real photography carrying all the emotional warmth so the interface itself can stay quiet and neutral. Headings are light-weight and fluid, never bold or shouting; the only loud element on a page is the letter-spaced uppercase eyebrow kicker. Emphasis inside a heading is carried by italic terracotta runs, never by bold or a second typeface.

This calm-editorial voice is deliberate: the brand exists to reduce health anxiety, not add to it, so nothing in the visual system is allowed to feel clinical-cold or alarmist. The one confirmed rejection is a second brand hue or decorative gradient — color is spent once, on the accent, and everywhere else stays neutral ink/muted/white.

The same base language (accent, neutrals, pill buttons, soft large-radius cards, three-tier hairline shadows) is shared verbatim across the marketing landing page and the member, doctor, and admin portals — src/member-v2/tokens.css states explicitly that landing tokens are the source of truth and portal tokens must stay in sync with it. Doctor and admin surfaces layer their own semantic (non-brand) colors on top for status and workflow states.
keyCharacteristics: Pure white surfaces, one warm terracotta accent, no second brand hue
Light-weight (400) fluid display type, italic-terracotta emphasis inside headings
Fully-round (999px) pills for every button and chip; soft large radii (18–34px) for cards and image frames
Three-tier hairline shadow system (border / border-hover / lift) — always soft, never hard
Native system-font stack everywhere; no webfont shipped
Real photography desaturated-and-dimmed at rest, warming to full color on hover

rules.name: The One Hue Rule
rules.body: The entire system has exactly one brand color — terracotta. No second decorative hue, no gradient palette, no purple. Portal-specific status colors (amber, red, green) are semantic workflow signals, not brand colors, and never appear on the marketing landing page.

rules.name: The Governed Extension Rule
rules.body: Doctor and admin portals may introduce new semantic colors for status/workflow (warnings, released state, alerts) on top of the shared base tokens, but must never introduce a new decorative or brand hue. Any new portal color must justify itself as a functional status signal.

rules.name: The Italic-Accent Emphasis Rule
rules.body: em inside any h1/h2/h3 renders italic in the accent color. This is the only way headings carry emphasis — never bold, never a second font.

rules.name: The One Uppercase Rule
rules.body: Sentence case everywhere except the eyebrow kicker. Never Title Case a headline, never write an all-caps sentence.

rules.name: The Hairline-Not-Border Rule
rules.body: Cards never use a visible solid border. Edges are defined by the Border shadow tier's 1px ring, kept at low opacity.

dos: Do keep terracotta (#b85d35) as the only brand accent; spend it deliberately (CTAs, emphasis, active state), not decoratively.
Do use fully-round pills (999px) for every button and chip, with no exceptions.
Do use the italic + accent-color pattern for emphasis inside headings — never bold, never a second typeface.
Do keep the three-tier hairline shadow system (Border / Border Hover / Lift) as the only elevation vocabulary — soft and low-contrast always.
Do let doctor/admin portals add semantic status colors (amber, red, green) for workflow states, governed by the One Hue Rule.
Do apply the grayscale-to-color hover treatment to photography, never to flat UI elements.
donts: Don't introduce a second brand/decorative hue, a gradient wash, or a purple — the accent stays singular.
Don't load a webfont; the system-font stack is a deliberate trust/speed signal, not a placeholder.
Don't use a visible solid border on a card; use the hairline shadow ring instead.
Don't use Title Case or ALL-CAPS on a headline — sentence case everywhere except the eyebrow kicker.
Don't promote a portal's semantic status color (doctor amber, admin green) onto the marketing landing page.
